messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: FastAPI, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: FastAPI
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: FastAPI, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: FastAPI\n- styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "project-init",
      "title": "Initialize project and Python environment",
      "description": "Create project folder, initialize a Python virtual environment, and install backend dependencies (fastapi, uvicorn, python-multipart if needed, and starlette for static files). Add a simple folder layout for app and static assets.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A repository directory exists with a virtualenv, requirements or poetry file listing FastAPI and Uvicorn, and an initial folder structure (app/, static/, templates/)."
    },
    {
      "id": "tailwind-setup",
      "title": "Set up Tailwind CSS build pipeline",
      "description": "Initialize npm in the project, install tailwindcss, postcss and autoprefixer, create tailwind.config.js and input CSS file, and add npm scripts to build/watch the CSS output into the static directory.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-init"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Node dependencies installed and npm scripts produce a built CSS file (e.g., static/css/tailwind.css) when running the build script, and tailwind.config.js is present."
    },
    {
      "id": "fastapi-app",
      "title": "Create FastAPI backend with static file serving and color API",
      "description": "Implement a FastAPI app (main.py) that mounts StaticFiles to serve the built frontend, and exposes a minimal JSON API with GET /api/color (returns current color) and POST /api/color (accepts {color: \"#rrggbb\"}) to update the color in memory or persistent store.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-init"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Uvicorn can run the app, static files are served at /, GET /api/color returns a JSON color, and POST /api/color updates the returned value."
    },
    {
      "id": "frontend-html",
      "title": "Build frontend UI with Tailwind and JavaScript",
      "description": "Create index.html in the static folder using Tailwind classes displaying the text 'hello world' and a UI for modifying its color (color input, hex input, and a few preset buttons). Add JavaScript to fetch current color from GET /api/color on load and to call POST /api/color when the user changes color, applying updates immediately.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "tailwind-setup",
        "fastapi-app"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Index page loads in browser, shows 'hello world', UI controls exist, initial color is fetched from the API and applied, and changing controls updates the text color in the page and triggers the POST request."
    },
    {
      "id": "static-build-integration",
      "title": "Integrate Tailwind build output and reference from frontend",
      "description": "Ensure index.html references the built Tailwind CSS file in static/css and that the build output path matches the static mount used by FastAPI. Adjust build scripts so production and dev workflows are straightforward.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "tailwind-setup",
        "frontend-html",
        "fastapi-app"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "index.html loads the built CSS from the server path (e.g., /static/css/tailwind.css), and styles render correctly in the browser after running the build script."
    },
    {
      "id": "persistence-optional",
      "title": "Add optional server-side persistence for color",
      "description": "Implement a simple persistence mechanism (JSON file like color.json or lightweight sqlite) so the selected color survives server restarts. Validate read/write logic and handle concurrent updates safely (atomic write).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "fastapi-app"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "After updating the color via the frontend and restarting the FastAPI server, GET /api/color returns the last saved color from the persistent store."
    },
    {
      "id": "run-and-test",
      "title": "Run dev environment and verify end-to-end functionality",
      "description": "Start the Tailwind watcher/build and start Uvicorn to serve the FastAPI app. Open the frontend in a browser, test fetching initial color, changing colors through the UI, and confirm API calls and persistence behavior (if implemented).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "static-build-integration",
        "persistence-optional"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Developer can run npm build/watch and uvicorn, open the app at http://localhost:<port>, change the 'hello world' text color via the UI, and observe immediate visual update plus API success responses."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: start by initializing the project environment, add the frontend styling toolchain (Tailwind), implement the backend API and static serving, build the frontend UI that interacts with the API, integrate the built CSS, optionally add persistence, and finish with running and testing the full dev workflow. Dependencies are ordered to avoid circular references and enable incremental testing."
}
